title: Benchmark recall against brute-force ground truth
description: Establish exact nearest neighbours on a sample, then measure how many the ANN index recovers at each setting.
body: Use when validating that an ANN index returns good-enough results. Actions: take a representative sample of query vectors, compute exact top-k with brute-force search to form ground truth, run the ANN index at candidate parameters, and compute recall@k as the fraction of true neighbours recovered. Sweep ef_search or nprobe and plot recall against latency to find the knee. Verify the chosen setting meets the recall target on a held-out query set. Validation signals include a recall-versus-latency curve and the production setting landing on the target. Risk boundary: recall measured on training queries overstates real performance, so use held-out queries and re-benchmark after any embedding-model or data change.
